title: Anthropic vows court fight in Pentagon row
description: Anthropic chief executive Dario Amodei has said the company has "no choice" but to challenge in court the Pentagon's formal designation of the artificial intelligence firm as a risk to US national security.
content:
WASHINGTON: Anthropic chief executive Dario Amodei has said the company has "no choice" but to challenge in court the Pentagon's formal designation of the artificial intelligence firm as a risk to US national security.

description: The Trump administration has drawn up ​strict rules for ‌civilian artificial intelligence contracts that would require AI ​companies to allow "any ​lawful" use of their ⁠models amid a stand-off ​between the Pentagon and ​Anthropic, the Financial Times reported on Friday.

title: Pentagon's chief tech officer says he clashed with AI company Anthropic over autonomous warfare
description: A top Pentagon official says a fight with Anthropic centered on how the military could someday use artificial intelligence in autonomous weapons. Defense Undersecretary Emil Michael told podcasters that his months-long negotiations with Anthropic included a debate over President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome missile defense plan. He says the Pentagon needs “all lawful use” terms, not case-by-case exceptions. Michael's remarks on the "All-In" podcast were his most extensive public commentary about the clash that led the Pentagon to designate Anthropic a supply chain risk this week. Anthropic has vowed to sue.
content: A top Pentagon official said Anthropic's dispute with the government over the use of its artificial intelligence technology in fully autonomous weapons came after a debate over how AI could be used in President Donald Trump's future Golden Dome missi... [1629 chars]

title: Oracle and OpenAI drop Texas data center expansion plan, Bloomberg News reports
description: March 6 (Reuters) - Oracle and OpenAI ⁠have abandoned plans to expand a flagship artificial intelligence data center ⁠in Texas after negotiations dragged over financing and OpenAI's changing needs, Bloomberg ‌News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.
